title: eriogonum latifolium subsp. typicum
intro: Eriogonum latifolium subsp. typicum, also known as the broad-leaved buckwheat, is a plant species in the buckwheat family (Polygonaceae) that is native to the western United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Eriogonum latifolium subsp. typicum include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and support cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and salves, although further research is needed to fully understand its effects and optimal uses.
